---
title: "Emmanuel Kubuafor, M.S, [Gstat](https://ww2.amstat.org/accreditation/roster.cfm)"
description-meta: "Academic website for Emmanuel Kubuafor, specializing in statistical modeling and data science."
image: "files/images/pic4.png"
page-navigation: true
toc: false
page-layout: full
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 50%
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:kubuafor@uw.edu
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/emmanuel-kubuafor-gstat-93b355263/
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< fa brands r-project >}} RPubs"
      href: https://rpubs.com/Emmanuel_Kubuafor
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=BXzI-m8AAAAJ&hl=en
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai researchgate >}} Research Gate"
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Emmanuel-Kubuafor-2?ev=hdr_xprf
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0000-7315-4041
      target: _blank
---

:::{#hero-heading}

## About Me

Thank you for visiting. I appreciate your time here.

I am a Statistician and Ph.D. student in Biostatistics at the [University of Washington](https://www.washington.edu). I completed my M.S. in Statistics at the [University of New Mexico](https://www.unm.edu) and my B.Sc. in Actuarial Science at the [University of Ghana](https://www.ug.edu.gh).

My research develops statistical methods for questions in health and population data, with an emphasis on work that is rigorous, practical, and relevant to clinical and policy decisions.

Teaching and mentoring matter to me as much as research. I teach and tutor students in quantitative coursework and mentor those from underrepresented backgrounds finding their footing in STEM. I am also actively involved with the [American Statistical Association](https://www.amstat.org) and ENAR ([Eastern North American Region](https://www.enar.org)), where I have served as a session chair, reviewed for student research programs, and taken part in education and early-career initiatives.

## Beyond the Numbers

Away from work, I like to cook (hopefully not burning anything while staring at the game), follow European football (yes, I argue about referees like it’s my job), and enjoy graphic design. I also love to travel and experience new cultures, which keeps me curious about the wider world and the different ways people make sense of it.

## Education

```{=html}
<div class="edu-timeline">
  <div class="edu-item edu-current">
    <div class="edu-degree">Ph.D. in Biostatistics</div>
    <div class="edu-school">University of Washington · <span class="edu-year">September 2026 — May 2030</span></div>
  </div>
  <div class="edu-item">
    <div class="edu-degree">M.S. in Statistics</div>
    <div class="edu-school">University of New Mexico · <span class="edu-year">Jan 2024 — May 2026</span></div>
  </div>
  <div class="edu-item">
    <div class="edu-degree">B.Sc. in Actuarial Science</div>
    <div class="edu-school">University of Ghana · <span class="edu-year">Aug 2018 — Sep 2022</span></div>
  </div>
</div>
```
